run_status: not yet run -- this file is the PRE-REGISTRATION, written before any checker call
question: Does routing (haiku attempts the task once; a checker ships or escalates; on escalate sonnet redoes the WHOLE task, no coaching) reach sonnet-solo quality at materially lower cost?

design.substrate: benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid's graded gradient subset (8 evals x 3 reps): the haiku-solo and sonnet-solo cells ARE the two halves of the routing arm (issue #109). The routing arm is COMPOSED, not re-executed: routing(eval,rep) = haiku-solo(eval,rep) if the checker says SHIP, else sonnet-solo(eval, SAME rep). Composition is exact for this design: escalation discards the haiku attempt entirely and sonnet redoes the task from the original request alone (no coaching), so there is no interaction between the halves that a live run would have and the simulation lacks.
design.new_spend: checker calls only: 24 haiku gradient cells x 2 model checker variants = 48 hermetic claude -p calls; the mechanized variant is offline ($0).

checker_variants.sonnet-judge: sonnet, sees the ORIGINAL REQUEST + the haiku deliverable ONLY (no eval expectations/rubric -- deployment-realistic), returns STRICT JSON {verdict: SHIP|ESCALATE, reason}. Prompt framing mirrors the fullgrid harness.py validate_prompt so the fidelity number is comparable to #41's 25% validator false-accept; decision semantics differ: SHIP/ESCALATE, no corrections requested (routing has no coaching).
checker_variants.haiku-judge: identical prompt, model haiku -- the cheap-checker variant.
checker_variants.mechanized: SHIP iff ALL of the eval's mechanized structural checks (benchmarks/lib/mechanized_checks.py, read from the fullgrid's graded/mechanized.csv) pass on the haiku cell; checker cost $0. CAVEAT: these checks were authored from the eval expectations, so this variant models the deployment case where task-type-specific deterministic lint checks are hand-written -- its fidelity is an upper bound on what rubric-informed checklists buy, not a rubric-free judge.

design.checker_parse_failure_rule: an unparseable checker response counts as ESCALATE (fail-safe) and is flagged in the composed record
design.escalation_pairing: escalated (eval,rep) takes sonnet-solo's SAME rep index -- deterministic, decided before any checker call

executor.vehicle: checker_harness.py (Python stdlib), same hermetic claude -p driver pattern as the fullgrid's harness.py, run inside WSL Debian
executor.hermeticity: clean CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR ($HOME/issue30/claude-config), --disallowedTools full deny list, prompt on stdin, empty scratch cwd outside the repo checkout (ADR 0023)

grading.no_new_grading: routing cell quality = the already-blind-graded frac-met of whichever half ships (met_final = min(grader, prosecutor) per ADR 0025, from the fullgrid's verdicts.jsonl + prosecute_counts.jsonl). Grades were assigned blind, before this experiment existed; the checker never sees grades; the composition is deterministic arithmetic.
grading.designer_knowledge_limitation: the aggregate outcome of both halves (haiku ~0.315, sonnet ~0.597 frac-met on the gradient battery) was already public in issue #109 before this pre-registration; per-cell grades were not consulted when authoring the checker prompts. Recorded as a limitation, not a blind.

metrics.headline_quality: fraction_met, eval-clustered mean delta routing_variant - sonnet-solo over the 8 gradient evals (reps averaged within eval, ADR 0019); 95% CI reported as secondary confidence, not gating (ADR 0027-style directional bar at n=8)
metrics.cost: real per-cell cost_usd from the substrate cells' claude -p envelopes; routing cell cost = haiku_cost + checker_cost + (escalated ? sonnet_cost(same rep) : 0). Median per-cell cost ratio vs sonnet-solo gates (issue #109's phrasing); mean/total cost ratio and composed sequential wall-clock ratio (haiku_wall + checker_wall + escalated x sonnet_wall) reported for context.
metrics.checker_fidelity: PRIMARY SECONDARY METRIC (issue #109's pre-registered threat). false-accept = checker says SHIP on a cell whose haiku frac_met < (that eval's sonnet-solo mean frac_met - 0.05), i.e. shipping it loses more than the non-inferiority margin vs escalating. Reported as rate over all 24 cells (comparable to #41's 6/24 = 25% validator figure) and as rate among accepts. Escalation rate E reported per variant.

adoption_bar.per_variant: ADOPT routing(variant) iff ALL THREE: (1) quality: mean_delta(routing_variant - sonnet-solo) > -0.05 on fraction-met (point estimate, eval-clustered over the 8 gradient evals); (2) cost: median per-cell cost ratio (routing_variant / sonnet-solo) <= 0.6; (3) checker fidelity: false-accept rate over all 24 cells <= 0.15 (the issue's ~10-15% bound, taken at its permissive end).
adoption_bar.multiplicity: all 3 variants are evaluated against the same pre-registered bar; if more than one clears, adopt the cheapest (lowest median cost ratio). Small multiplicity (3 pre-registered variants) accepted and disclosed.
adoption_bar.kill_rule: if NO variant achieves false-accept <= 0.15, routing cannot reach sonnet-solo quality on this battery; record the null without further work (issue #109's pre-registered decision rule).

limitations: n=8 clustered evals -- same graded gradient subset and directional-bar rationale as ADR 0035

single-shot text tasks are the pessimal granularity for orchestration overhead (ADR 0035 [unverifiable]); routing's checker overhead is one call, so the granularity critique is weaker here but still applies to generalization

gradient battery selects tasks where haiku LOSES to sonnet -- exactly the population where a checker must escalate; an in-production task mix with more haiku-parity tasks would mechanically favor routing on cost. The verdict binds this battery only.
